channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Film academy apologizes for not naming 'No Other Land' co-director in response to attack on him
item.description: The organization that bestows the Academy Awards has apologized to “No Other Land” co-director Hamdan Ballal after mounting criticism following its response to the violent attack on the Oscar winner.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll -- above 140 -- was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, a bridge and a dam. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, the head of the military government said in the televised speech.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief urged regional and international leaders Friday to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war and speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration moves to eliminate USAID, firing remaining employees
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to formally end the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), notifying the remaining employees they will be terminated and the agency will be merged with the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel launched an attack on the Lebanese capital Beirut on Friday for the first time since a ceasefire ended fighting between Israeli forces and the Hezbollah militant group in November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, The Hill

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: A powerful earthquake has rocked Myanmar and caused extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tight-lipped following Hegseth, Vance 'loathing' text exchange
item.description: European leaders remain tight-lipped after "loathing" comments were issued by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance in leaked text exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, after 38 days battling a life-threatening case of pneumonia in both lungs, his doctors said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC
